title: [app] separate link and file sharing in app details
message:
[app] separate link and file sharing in app details

previously only file sharing worked and link sharing was broken.

Users were often confused why the file was shared which isn't something we want to encourage, so now file sharing is a dedicated less prominent option.

title: [app] fix app share URI construction
message:
[app] fix app share URI construction

and introduce 'packages' suffix that gets appended to the base URI

title: Use junit test reports, so it is easier to see which test failed
message: Use junit test reports, so it is easier to see which test failed

title: [app] update auto-fetch label to show that apps also get installed
message:
[app] update auto-fetch label to show that apps also get installed

when session installer is available

title: [db] return the repoId of the archive when adding/enabling it
message: [db] return the repoId of the archive when adding/enabling it

title: [app] Give AppUpdateStatusManager the responsibility to queue app updates
message:
[app] Give AppUpdateStatusManager the responsibility to queue app updates

This is mostly for efficiency, since it queries the DB for new updates anyway, so it can already enqueue those for auto-update if the respective settings allow.

title: [app] add RepoUpdateManager and RepoUpdateWorker
message:
[app] add RepoUpdateManager and RepoUpdateWorker

to replace UpdateService which is still based on deprecated JobService API and known to be unreliable.

This also introduces a new lean NotificationManager which can eventually supersede the old one.

title: [app] replace all usages of UpdateService with new alternatives
message:
[app] replace all usages of UpdateService with new alternatives

This uses LiveData to replace local broadcasts. One advantage of this is that the last live data value doesn't get lost when the view was stopped.

title: [app] show earliest next update check in the UI
message:
[app] show earliest next update check in the UI

This is useful for debugging scheduling issues. The user can immediately see if and when an update is scheduled to happen.

title: [app] Add new RepoUpdateReceiver for system apps to trigger repository updates
message:
[app] Add new RepoUpdateReceiver for system apps to trigger repository updates

One known use-case for this is to do an initial repository update during SetupWizard, so app data is available when needed, e.g. for restoring app backups.

title: [app] Remove unused UpdateService and related classes
message:
[app] Remove unused UpdateService and related classes

All functionality was migrated to its new WorkManager based replacement: RepoUpdateWorker and RepoUpdateManager

title: [db] Maintain legacy repo priority ordering when migrating
message: [db] Maintain legacy repo priority ordering when migrating

title: [download] disable tests using howsmyssl.com on Android 7 and older
message:
[download] disable tests using howsmyssl.com on Android 7 and older

because of letsencrypt not working anymore on those devices

title: [db] Maintain legacy repo priority ordering when migrating
message: [db] Maintain legacy repo priority ordering when migrating

title: [index] stop passing certificates around
message: [index] stop passing certificates around

title: [db] make repo certs non-null and remove repos without cert
message:
[db] make repo certs non-null and remove repos without cert

historically, repos were added to the DB without much information and could stay in a broken state until manually removed. If a repo is updating, it should have a cert. So only repos that never did a single index update don't have a cert. Nowadays, this can not happen anymore as we get the repo and its cert before adding it to the DB. So whenever we update a repo, we know its certificate and fingerprint. Thus, this includes a DB migration removing all broken repos and making the certificate for repos in the DB non-null.

title: [app] Give AppUpdateStatusManager the responsibility to queue app updates
message:
[app] Give AppUpdateStatusManager the responsibility to queue app updates

This is mostly for efficiency, since it queries the DB for new updates anyway, so it can already enqueue those for auto-update if the respective settings allow.

title: [app] add RepoUpdateManager and RepoUpdateWorker
message:
[app] add RepoUpdateManager and RepoUpdateWorker

to replace UpdateService which is still based on deprecated JobService API and known to be unreliable.

This also introduces a new lean NotificationManager which can eventually supersede the old one.

title: [app] replace all usages of UpdateService with new alternatives
message:
[app] replace all usages of UpdateService with new alternatives

This uses LiveData to replace local broadcasts. One advantage of this is that the last live data value doesn't get lost when the view was stopped.

title: [app] show earliest next update check in the UI
message:
[app] show earliest next update check in the UI

This is useful for debugging scheduling issues. The user can immediately see if and when an update is scheduled to happen.

title: [app] Add new RepoUpdateReceiver for system apps to trigger repository updates
message:
[app] Add new RepoUpdateReceiver for system apps to trigger repository updates

One known use-case for this is to do an initial repository update during SetupWizard, so app data is available when needed, e.g. for restoring app backups.

title: [app] Remove unused UpdateService and related classes
message:
[app] Remove unused UpdateService and related classes

All functionality was migrated to its new WorkManager based replacement: RepoUpdateWorker and RepoUpdateManager

title: [app] separate link and file sharing in app details
message:
[app] separate link and file sharing in app details

previously only file sharing worked and link sharing was broken.

Users were often confused why the file was shared which isn't something we want to encourage, so now file sharing is a dedicated less prominent option.

message:

and introduce 'packages' suffix that gets appended to the base URI

message: [db] Maintain legacy repo priority ordering when migrating

title: [download] disable tests using howsmyssl.com on Android 7 and older
message:
[download] disable tests using howsmyssl.com on Android 7 and older

because of letsencrypt not working anymore on those devices

title: [db] Maintain legacy repo priority ordering when migrating
message: [db] Maintain legacy repo priority ordering when migrating

title: [index] stop passing certificates around
message: [index] stop passing certificates around

title: [db] make repo certs non-null and remove repos without cert
message:
[db] make repo certs non-null and remove repos without cert

historically, repos were added to the DB without much information and could stay in a broken state until manually removed. If a repo is updating, it should have a cert. So only repos that never did a single index update don't have a cert. Nowadays, this can not happen anymore as we get the repo and its cert before adding it to the DB. So whenever we update a repo, we know its certificate and fingerprint. Thus, this includes a DB migration removing all broken repos and making the certificate for repos in the DB non-null.
